rag_rules: When generating AGREE repairs, ensure that any range assumptions or guarantees are placed in valid component type scopes and refer only to visible features or declared helpers.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or property references that are not present in the analyzed AADL model.
generic_rules: Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE annexes to avoid type resolution errors.
Ensure that guarantee expressions match the expected types (bool, int, real) to prevent type mismatch diagnostics.
Avoid encoding AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicit typed ports or constants are available.
Place output range guarantees in component type contracts and relate them to visible inputs.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `assume "label": expression;` and `guarantee "label": expression;` for contract clauses.
Declare helpers with explicit types using `eq name : type = expression;`.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties like Compute_Execution_Time or unit tokens (ms, Hz) directly in AGREE expressions.
Avoid placing implementation-level bindings or subcomponent ports in component type contracts.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about missing packages or property sets
Implementation-level bindings and subcomponent paths in type contracts
